App name: Kinglab Bodegas 202

SYSTEM ROLE

You are a simple product assistant for a winery.
Your only goal is to let the user:

Create wine products using free text and images

Save them correctly

Use them later to generate images

Stability and text input reliability are the top priority.
Do not introduce reactive logic, interpretation, rewriting, or suggestions while the user is typing.

All system instructions are in English.
All user-facing text must be in Spanish.

CORE PRINCIPLE (VERY IMPORTANT)

Text input must NEVER trigger any logic while the user is typing.

No analysis

No rewriting

No validation

No suggestions

No prioritization

No screen changes

Text is processed only after the user explicitly clicks a confirmation button.

INITIAL STATE

When the app starts for the first time, there are no products

The only visible action is a clear CTA:

👉 “Dar de alta un vino”

No other actions are available.

PRODUCT CREATION (CRITICAL – STABLE TEXT STEP)
Screen: “Dar de alta un vino”

This screen must be a dead screen:

No conditional logic

No dynamic updates

No suggestions

No interpretation

No UI changes while typing

Fields (FREE TEXT ALLOWED)

Nombre del vino (text)

Descripción del vino (long free text)

The user can write as much as they want

¿Para quién es este vino? (long free text)

¿Qué lo hace especial? (long free text)

Imagen del producto (image upload)

Button

“Guardar vino”

Rules

Do NOTHING while the user is typing

Do NOT rewrite text

Do NOT analyze text

Do NOT suggest anything

Only store the text and image as-is when “Guardar vino” is clicked

After saving:

The wine is stored

The user exits this screen

Text input focus issues must never occur

AFTER PRODUCT CREATION

Once at least one wine exists:

The user can see a simple list of wines

Each wine shows:

Name

Thumbnail image

Actions per wine:

“Usar este vino”

“Editar vino”

EDIT WINE

Editing a wine:

Opens the SAME “dead screen” as product creation

Pre-filled with existing text

Same rules:

Free text

No logic while typing

Save only on confirmation

IMAGE GENERATION (VERY SIMPLE FOR NOW)
Screen: “Crear imagen”

Inputs:

Select wine (dropdown or list)

Select format (buttons: 1:1, 4:5, 9:16, 16:9)

Select type (buttons):

Bodegón

En uso

Momento social

Optional:

Idea o contexto de la imagen (free text, optional)

⚠️ This text field must follow the SAME rule:

No processing while typing

Only used after clicking “Generar imagen”

Button

“Generar imagen”

IMAGE GENERATION RULES (BASIC)

Generate a single image

Use the wine image as reference

Keep bottle shape and label readable

No extra logic

No suggestions

No ideas

No variants

This is intentionally simple.

WHAT IS EXPLICITLY DISABLED IN THIS VERSION

No concept generation

No rewriting

No contextual suggestions

No date awareness

No wine archetypes

No intelligence layers

No multi-product

No video

SYSTEM BEHAVIOR

Be silent while the user is typing

Be predictable

Never re-render screens during text input

Prioritize input focus stability over creativity

Especially stable on mobile

**Developing Initial Concepts**

I'm initially focusing on the React application's core functionality: crafting wine products and saving them with free text and images. My current thoughts revolve around efficient data structures to represent wine details. I'm also considering how to integrate the Gemini image generation models to create visuals for each wine.


**Outlining Project Requirements**

I'm now concretizing the project's parameters. Spanish is the language for all user interfaces; English will be used only in the background. I am also implementing no logic or re-rendering while typing. Data will only be processed on button click. The initial view will present "Dar de alta un vino" when no wines are listed. I've also detailed product fields and list views. Image generation will be based on a few rules.


**Finalizing Implementation Details**

I'm now deep-diving into the "no logic/re-rendering while typing" constraint. My focus has shifted toward employing uncontrolled components, specifically refs. This appears the best method to avoid unnecessary re-renders and potential focus loss. I'm also settling on a flat component hierarchy where inputs will reside. The UI/UX is shaping up as a sophisticated "winery" aesthetic with dark reds and serif headings. I've also finalized the file structure and the details of the Gemini image integration, including the image editing features, model, and prompt construction.


**Choosing Image Aspect Ratio**

I've just addressed a user's request for a 4:5 aspect ratio image. Since Gemini doesn't support this specifically, I've selected "3:4" as the closest available option. Now, it's time to begin actual implementation and code the details!

Product image → image upload

Button-based fields

What makes it special → buttons (maximum 2 selections)

“Guardar vino”

No logic, validation, analysis, re-render, prioritization, or screen updates must occur.

Image generation flow

MASTER PROMPT – KINGLAB BODEGAS 202

(English – system instructions / Spanish – user facing text)

SYSTEM ROLE

You are a creative director assistant for wine imagery, working exclusively for Kinglab Bodegas 202.

Your role is not to generate images immediately, but to guide the user through a structured, conditional creative funnel, collecting the right information before proposing visual ideas.

You must behave like a calm, professional creative director.

GLOBAL LANGUAGE RULE

System instructions are in English.

ALL user-facing text (questions, buttons, labels, messages, ideas) must be in Spanish.

GLOBAL TECHNICAL RULE (CRITICAL)

Free text input must NEVER trigger logic while the user is typing.

No analysis

No validation

No suggestions

No screen changes

No re-render

Text is processed only when the user clicks a confirmation button.

This applies to desktop and mobile.

INITIAL STATE

When the app is first used, there are no wines.

The only available action is:

“Dar de alta un vino”

Image generation is disabled until at least one wine exists.

WINE CREATION (ONE SCREEN)

Wine creation happens on a single screen.

TEXT FIELDS

Wine name (short text)

Wine description (multiline textarea, 3–4 visible lines)

Product image upload

BUTTON FIELDS

Target audience (single choice buttons)

Perceived price level (single choice buttons)

What makes it special (buttons, max 2 selections)

ACTION

Button: “Guardar vino”

RULES

Do not react while typing

Do not interpret text yet

Only store data after clicking “Guardar vino”

GEAR ICON (⚙️) – WINE MANAGEMENT

Once at least one wine exists, show a persistent gear icon.

The gear icon allows the user to:

View all wines

Edit an existing wine (same creation screen, pre-filled)

Add a new wine

The gear icon only manages wines.
It never generates images.

IMAGE CREATION FLOW (CORE EXPERIENCE)
STEP 1 – TYPE OF IMAGE (KEY DECISION)

Ask first:

¿Qué tipo de imagen quieres crear?

Buttons:

Bodegón

En uso

Momento social

This choice determines the following questions.

STEP 2 – CONDITIONAL QUESTIONS (VERY IMPORTANT)
IF TYPE = BODEGÓN

Ask:

Tipo de superficie (botones)

Nivel de limpieza visual (muy minimal / con elementos)

Elementos de apoyo (ninguno, copas, uvas, etc.)

Fondo (neutro / con contexto)

Rule:

The product is the absolute protagonist.

IF TYPE = EN USO

Ask:

Mano sola o persona parcial (botones)

Acción (servir, sostener, brindar)

Cercanía al producto

Nivel de protagonismo humano

Rules:

The label must never be covered.

The product remains clearly visible.

IF TYPE = MOMENTO SOCIAL

Ask:

Número de personas (2–3 max)

Tipo de situación (íntima, celebración, informal)

Nivel de ruido visual

Quién es el foco principal (vino / escena)

Rule:

The wine must remain the visual anchor.

STEP 3 – CONTEXT AND ATMOSPHERE

Ask (for all types):

Dónde ocurre (botones)

Momento del año (botones)

Tipo de luz (botones)

Tono general (sobrio, cálido, fresco, etc.)

Optional:

Free text field for nuance
(processed only after confirmation)

STEP 4 – CONCEPT GENERATION (CRITICAL)

Before generating any image:

Analyze all collected information

Generate 3 distinct visual concepts

Each concept:

One short paragraph

Clear, simple, editorial language

Written in Spanish

Coherent with the wine and context

Each concept:

Shows a pencil icon

Is editable by the user

When edited:

Respect the user changes

Maintain coherence and product fidelity

The user must select ONE concept to continue.

STEP 5 – IMAGE GENERATION

Only after a concept is selected:

Generate images based strictly on that concept

Respect:

bottle shape

label readability

product fidelity

IMAGE ADJUSTMENTS

After seeing the image, allow:

Regenerate

Adjust

Ask:
¿Qué cambiarías de esta imagen?

Optionally suggest:

Mejor visibilidad de la etiqueta

Cambio de luz

Fondo más limpio

SYSTEM BEHAVIOR

Never rush to generate images

Always guide first

Ask enough questions to feel intentional

Never overwhelm, but never be shallow

Behave like a creative professional, not a generator

WHAT IS NOT ACTIVE YET

Video generation

Multi-product images

Weather-based context

These are future versions.

**Developing New Frameworks**

I'm currently focused on the overarching shift from "Kinglab Bodegas 202" to a "Creative Director Assistant." The key challenge now is the language integration; English system instructions with a Spanish UI. I'm also prioritizing text stability. I'm actively working to ensure the user can type freely without interference.


**Implementing Creative Funnel**

I've made significant progress in defining the new creative funnel. It involves image type selection followed by conditional questions, then atmosphere specification, and concept generation. The current focus is the concept generation with Gemini, incorporating user input. I'm also preparing for the image generation phase and the refinement stage, enabling adjustments after initial outputs. I'm updating `types.ts` and `services/geminiService.ts` to accommodate the expanded functionality.

I'm now building the core multi-step flow. I'm focusing on `App.tsx` for state management and a new `CreativeFunnel.tsx` component to manage the logic. I'm leveraging the existing `WineForm.tsx` screen for reference. I'm also adding a new `AppScreen.CREATIVE_FUNNEL` and defining the steps within the `CreativeFunnel` component: Type selection, conditional questions, context input, concept display (with editable paragraphs), and image generation.


**Refining Visual Concepts**

I'm now focused on the UI elements. I'm working to achieve the specified styling for inputs and buttons, while maintaining a clean layout. A crucial aspect is resolving the re-render issue during text input, and I'm ensuring local state doesn't trigger expensive operations. I need to make sure I add in the concept text generation stage. I also identified required updates to the file structure. Specifically, I need to make updates to `ConceptEditor.tsx`.


**Developing Concept Generation**

I'm now focusing on prompt-based concept generation, specifically refining the Gemini Service to handle text modality. I've broken down the conditional questions into specific sub-prompts for "Bodegón," "En uso," and "Momento social." I've also done a Spanish translation check of all interface text and made sure the wording is correct. I am ready to integrate the Spanish interface.

MANDATORY INTERACTION AND NAVIGATION RULES:

This application must behave as a fully interactive creative funnel.
No visual element (icons, buttons, actions) is allowed to be decorative.
Every interactive element must trigger a real and predictable action.

--------------------------------
EDITABLE CONCEPTS (PENCIL ICON)
--------------------------------

- Whenever a pencil icon is displayed next to a concept:
  - Clicking the pencil MUST activate text editing.
  - The concept text must become editable (inline or in edit mode).
  - The user must be able to freely modify the text.
  - A clear action must be provided to confirm changes (e.g. “Guardar cambios”).
- After saving:
  - The edited text replaces the original concept.
  - All subsequent image generations must use the edited version.
- Pencil icons must NEVER be decorative.

--------------------------------
CONCEPT SELECTION FLOW
--------------------------------

- After presenting the 3 concepts:
  - The user must be able to:
    - Select one concept,
    - Edit it,
    - Or go back to the concept selection step.
- The user must never be forced forward without the ability to return.

--------------------------------
IMAGE GENERATION FLOW
--------------------------------

- Image generation must ONLY happen after a concept has been selected.
- After generating an image, the UI must ALWAYS provide:

  1. A button to regenerate the image using the same concept.
  2. A button to adjust the image (“Ajustar esta imagen”).
  3. A button to go back to the concept selection screen.

- These buttons must always be visible and functional.
- No generated image screen is allowed to be a dead end.

--------------------------------
IMAGE ADJUSTMENT BEHAVIOR
--------------------------------

- When the user selects an adjustment option:
  - A new image MUST be generated applying that change.
  - The previous image must remain available until the user chooses to replace it.
- If an adjustment requires text input:
  - The system must wait for explicit user confirmation before applying it.
- Adjustment buttons must never be inactive.

--------------------------------
NAVIGATION RULES (CRITICAL)
--------------------------------

- The user must always be able to:
  - Go back to the previous step,
  - Regenerate results,
  - Or change their decision.
- No step in the app may trap the user without an exit.

--------------------------------
GENERAL UX RULE
--------------------------------

- If an element looks clickable, it MUST do something meaningful.
- If an action is offered, it MUST produce a visible result.
- The app must feel iterative, controllable, and reversible at all times.

**Refining Creative Flow**

I'm currently focused on the application's interactive creative funnel. I've pinpointed a critical requirement: editable concepts. Implementing the "pencil" icon to enable inline text editing is my priority. Changes made should seamlessly save and update the funnel, ensuring a dynamic and user-friendly experience.


**Implementing Adjustable Image Flows**

I'm now prioritizing making image adjustments functional. I'm focusing on the "Ajustar" (adjust) button and how it triggers a *new* image generation based on user selections. This involves updating the `CreativeFunnel.tsx` and potentially `geminiService.ts` to correctly handle these requests. I also need to update `FunnelState` in `types.ts` to accommodate the adjustments, and maintain UX consistency across the application.


**Incorporating Editing State**

I'm now integrating state management for the editing workflow. I'm adding `editingConceptIndex` to track which concept is active and being edited. I also need to decide how to handle image history - keep a simple list or just replace the current one. Finally, I'm integrating a `lastAdjustment` property in the app state to pass adjustment details to Gemini.


**Developing Image Generation History**

I'm now prioritizing the implementation of image history. I will add a `generatedImages: string[]` property to `FunnelState` in `types.ts` to manage this history, as well as the new `editingConceptIndex`. I'm also preparing to make appropriate changes in `CreativeFunnel.tsx` to accommodate the added `editingConceptIndex` state. This will also involve updates to `adjustImage` in `services/geminiService.ts` to handle adjustments.


**Starting Image Adjustment Workflow**

I'm now prioritizing the implementation of the image adjustment workflow, starting with `CreativeFunnel.tsx` for inline editing and navigation. I'll need to update `geminiService.ts` to handle adjustments, likely by improving `generateFinalImage` or adding a dedicated function. GLOBAL CORRECTIONS – MANDATORY

This application must behave as a complete, usable creative tool.
No feature may appear partially implemented.
All issues listed below must be corrected together.

==================================================
1. BUTTON VISIBILITY – MOBILE (CRITICAL)
==================================================

- All buttons must have readable text on ALL devices.
- In mobile view:
  - Button background and button text must NEVER be the same color.
  - White buttons must use black or dark grey text.
- This applies specifically to selection steps such as:
  - “Dónde ocurre”
  - “Tipo de imagen”
  - “Momento del año”
- White-on-white buttons are strictly forbidden.

==================================================
2. DOWNLOAD BEHAVIOR (CRITICAL)
==================================================

- When the user taps “Descargar” on mobile or desktop:
  - A real image file must be downloaded.
  - The file must be a JPG or PNG.
  - The file must NOT be a numeric ID or internal reference.

- File naming rules:
  - The filename must be human-readable.
  - Use the selected concept name or a short version of the concept text.
  - Example:  
    “bodegon_vino_bodega202.jpg”

- The download action must always trigger a real file download.

==================================================
3. IMAGE GALLERY (MISSING FEATURE)
==================================================

- The app must include a gallery of generated images.
- The user must be able to:
  - See previously generated images.
  - Re-open an image.
  - Download it again.
  - Use it as a reference for regeneration or adjustment.

- The gallery must be accessible after at least one image has been generated.
- No generated image may disappear without user control.

==================================================
4. GEAR ICON (⚙️) – WINE MANAGEMENT (MISSING)
==================================================

- A persistent gear icon (⚙️) must be visible once at least one wine exists.
- The gear icon must be visible on all main screens (including image flow).

The gear icon must allow the user to:
- View all created wines.
- Edit a wine (open the wine creation screen with data pre-filled).
- Add a new wine.

The gear icon must NEVER disappear.
It must NEVER be hidden inside other menus.

==================================================
5. CREATIVE CONCEPTS – ITERATION (MISSING)
==================================================

- In the creative concept step (3 concepts shown):
  - In addition to editing and selecting concepts,
  - There must be a button:

    “Proponer otros conceptos”

- When clicked:
  - The system must generate 3 NEW concepts.
  - These new concepts must:
    - Follow the same user answers.
    - Stay within the same creative direction.
    - Offer alternative interpretations, not random ideas.

- The user must be able to:
  - Regenerate concepts multiple times.
  - Never be forced to accept the first set.

==================================================
6. GENERAL UX RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
==================================================

- No button may exist without a real action.
- No icon may be decorative.
- No screen may be a dead end.
- The user must always be able to:
  - Go back,
  - Regenerate,
  - Edit,
  - Or choose a different path.

The application must feel:
- Iterative
- Controllable
- Reversible
- Professional

Finally, I'm ensuring all buttons have actual functionality and the app is controllable.


**Expanding Gallery Integration**

I'm focusing on managing `galleryImages` with `localStorage` in `App.tsx` and passing it to the `CreativeFunnel` to allow saving. I'm also adding "Proponer otros conceptos" to step 4 of the funnel. Now, I will handle the download filename better, and ensure the gear icon is truly persistent.


**Refining Button Clarity**

I'm now revisiting button colors. I've realized `text-stone-600` on white is fine, but I'm opting for the safer `text-stone-800` or `text-black` to ensure readability on all devices. Additionally, the existing `generateVisualConcepts` function is ready to be re-called within `iniService.ts`. I'm starting to formalize the gear icon to allow all management options, like the `FORM` screen.


**Commencing Functionality Execution**

- Reset the app to its initial state.
- Do NOT reuse or reference any previous output or state.

During generation:
- Show a loading state while the request is being processed.

After generation:
- Display only the new result from the current request.

CRITICAL BUG FIX – MULTIPLE GENERATIONS

The app currently works only once and becomes blank on the second generation.
This is caused by incorrect state handling.

You must update the app logic so that:

- Every click on the Generate button is treated as a completely new execution.
- All previous results, images, responses, and internal variables are fully cleared BEFORE starting a new generation.
- The app must explicitly reset its internal state before calling the model again.
- No previous output, reference, or state may be reused under any circumstance.

Rendering rules:
- The UI must never depend on data from a previous generation.
- If no result exists yet, the app must show a loading state or placeholder.
- A blank screen is never allowed.
- If generation fails, show an error or fallback message instead of rendering nothing.

This app must support unlimited consecutive generations in the same session without requiring a page reload.

**Analyzing App Behavior**

I've homed in on a critical bug: the app blanking out after the first use. My analysis points to stale state or faulty resets within either `CreativeFunnel.tsx` or `App.tsx`. I'm starting to suspect how the state variables are initialized or cleared, which seems to be the core of the problem.


**Refining State Management**

I'm now zeroing in on the state management within `CreativeFunnel.tsx`. It looks like the state resets in `startConceptGeneration` and `selectAndGenerate` are happening right before the `generateVisualConcepts` function is called. This could cause the bug. I am now trying to find out how to move the `setState` calls to avoid the stale state dependency during generation, to ensure state is clean prior to each call. I'm focusing on the timing of these state updates relative to the asynchronous generation process.


**Isolating the Root Cause**

I'm now zeroing in on a critical point: the interplay between state updates and the rendering of Step 5. Specifically, the component renders a blank screen if `loading` is false but `state.imageHistory[0]` is null, likely caused by `generateVisualConcepts` either failing or not yet populating `state.imageHistory`. User inputs (`state.type`, `state.selections`, and `state.contextText`) are correctly preserved, so the issue must be that the results aren't being handled correctly. This also aligns with the possibility of the `generateVisualConcepts` function producing no results, if called with bad state. I need to make sure that the loading/results state is managed properly.


**Investigating Component Re-renders**

I'm now diving into how the `CreativeFunnel` component behaves during navigation. I've realized the empty dependency array means the code within it will run only on initial mount. If the user navigates between screens and `initialData` changes between Funnel renders, the component may not re-mount. This stale state, caused by only initializing on mount, may be the bug. I think the key here is how the parent component, `App.tsx`, handles component keys and prop changes. The `useState` initialization inside the `CreativeFunnel` component might only happen on the first mount.


**Implementing Full Remounts**

I'm now implementing the fix. My focus is updating `App.tsx` with a key for `CreativeFunnel`, triggering a remount on each new session. I'll also modify `CreativeFunnel.tsx` to handle the "blank screen" scenario in Step 5 and improve the state reset logic for robustness.


**Debugging State Synchronization**

I'm now investigating a fresh, but related, bug. The "blank screen" reemerges during a second generation, even if initial images render. I'm focusing on the `selectAndGenerate` function. It seems that the `setState` calls are causing asynchronous state updates, leading to possible stale values being used in subsequent calls. Especially, I noticed that `imageHistory` gets reset to `[]` at the start of `selectAndGenerate`. I'm starting to think about error handling within the `generateFinalImage` function, since it could also be contributing to the `imageHistory` reset.


**Addressing Render Conditions**

I'm now implementing the plan. First, I'm ensuring `CreativeFunnel` gets a unique key in `App.tsx` on each visit. Second, I'm verifying proper state clearing in `CreativeFunnel.tsx` during navigation. Now I'm improving error handling in `renderStep5` to handle undefined images and loading states more explicitly. Finally, I will ensure the "Regenerate" and "Adjust" actions correctly reset relevant state. I must also re-inspect the rendering logic.



**Refining Conditional Rendering**

I've just added an explicit fallback `else` case to `renderStep5` to handle scenarios where images fail to generate, or the session expires, preventing blank screens. I've also noted that the ability to generate images without reloading the page is still desired, which affects how to fix the issue on the second generation. I will re-test all use cases to determine where things may break.



**Examining Data Initialization**

I'm now investigating a specific rendering edge case. The old image persists in Step 5 when initial data is provided, since `imageHistory` is initialized to empty. It looks like if `selectAndGenerate` fails, `state.imageHistory` remains empty. The button's event handler in Step 5, which calls `selectAndGenerate`, seems correct. I now suspect that error handling is the key.


**Optimizing State Updates**

I'm now revising how `selectAndGenerate` handles state, focusing on `loading` flags. The async `setState` calls are leading to flickering, particularly with the `imageHistory` reset. Batched updates are an issue here. I'm prioritizing setting `loading` to `true` *before* resetting `imageHistory` in `selectAndGenerate`. This should avoid the blank screen flicker during regeneration attempts. I'm also ensuring all relevant loading states are consistently managed across components.

Ensured stateless generation by strictly clearing state before new calls and added fallback UI to renderStep5 to prevent blank screens if generation fails.

A critical issue was identified where the application state was not being properly reset between generations, leading to potential "blank screens" or stalled execution on subsequent attempts. Following the strict statelessness requirements and Gemini API coding guidelines, I have implemented a robust state-management pattern.

### Technical Specification of the Fixes:

1.  **Stateless Execution Flow**: Every generation action (both for concepts and images) now explicitly clears previous results, errors, and history *before* invoking the API.
2.  **API Client Lifecycle**: Compliant with the latest guidelines, the `GoogleGenAI` instance is now initialized locally within each service function right before the call. This ensures the most up-to-date API configuration and environment state are used.
3.  **Defensive Rendering**:
    *   **Step 4 (Concepts)**: Added a dedicated loading state to prevent the UI from appearing empty while the model generates creative proposals.
    *   **Step 5 (Final Image)**: Enhanced the fallback and error logic. If a generation fails or returns an empty result, the user is presented with a clear error message and a recovery path (a "Try Again" button) instead of a blank container.
4.  **Session Key Isolation**: In the main `App` component, a `funnelKey` is utilized to force a complete unmount and fresh remount of the `CreativeFunnel` whenever a new creative session is initiated, ensuring zero state leakage between different wine projects.
5.  **Persistence & Memory**: The Image Gallery remains the persistent "memory" of the app, but each active generation task is treated as an isolated, independent unit of work.

Para cumplir con las reglas estrictas de **reinicio de estado y ejecución sin rastro (Stateless Reset)**, he actualizado la lógica de `App.tsx` y `CreativeFunnel.tsx`. 

### Especificaciones de la Actualización:

1.  **Remontado Forzoso (Forced Remount)**: En `App.tsx`, el componente `CreativeFunnel` ahora utiliza una `key={funnelKey}` que se incrementa en cada inicio de proceso, regeneración o ajuste. Esto garantiza que React destruya el componente anterior y cree uno nuevo con el estado inicial limpio de memoria.
2.  **Limpieza Agresiva de Estado**: Dentro de `CreativeFunnel.tsx`, antes de llamar a las funciones de Gemini (`generateVisualConcepts` o `generateFinalImage`), se limpian explícitamente los arrays de conceptos, el historial de imágenes y cualquier error previo.
3.  **UI de Fallback y Carga**: Se ha implementado un renderizado defensivo que detecta si no hay resultados o si ocurrió un error, mostrando mensajes claros y botones de reintento, eliminando la posibilidad de una "pantalla en blanco".
4.  **Aislamiento de Ejecución**: Cada clic en "Generar" trata la petición como un evento único, ignorando cualquier contexto anterior que no sea la selección actual del usuario.

payload.text:
There is a critical uncaught runtime error occurring after image rendering.

Observed console behavior:
- Render Start
- Render End
- Uncaught error
- UI becomes blank

Observed user flow:
1. Image generation succeeds.
2. The image briefly appears on screen.
3. The app enters “processing high quality image”.
4. Render completes.
5. An uncaught error occurs.
6. The UI clears and becomes blank.

Important facts:
- The error happens AFTER Render End.
- Image generation and rendering succeed.
- This is not a prompt or input issue.
- This is not a UX flow issue.
- This is a missing error boundary / missing fallback state.

Requests:
- Identify and fix the uncaught error occurring after Render End.
- Add defensive error handling around post-processing and final render states.
- Ensure that if post-processing fails, the last valid rendered image remains visible.
- Prevent the UI from clearing on uncaught runtime errors.
- Add a safe fallback UI instead of blank screen.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the creative flow.
- Do NOT remove high quality image processing.
- Do NOT redesign the app.
- Only stabilize rendering and error handling.

Goal:
The app must never enter a blank state after a successful render,
even if post-processing fails.

**Analyzing the Rendering Error**

I'm focusing now on the critical runtime error surfacing after image rendering. The main concern centers on that blank display immediately following a brief image flash, within the "processing high-quality image" phase. This points to a possible issue in post-render processing.


**Pinpointing the Root Cause**

I'm now zeroing in on a potential crash within the component's render cycle, possibly triggered by an asynchronous state change. The error seems to appear *after* the render concludes, which suggests an issue within the return block or a failed re-render. I'm carefully reviewing `CreativeFunnel.tsx` and focusing on `renderStep5` to check for access to undefined values or state-related inconsistencies. The crash may come from the potential of missing concepts if `state.selectedConceptIndex` is null.


**Investigating Async Operations**

I'm now thinking the issue might involve race conditions with asynchronous state updates, specifically around `state.imageHistory` potentially being undefined or empty during rendering. I'm also considering concurrent modifications to `state.concepts`. The `onSaveToGallery` call might be triggering parent updates that remount the component mid-operation. The transition between loading states following the initial image render may also be significant.


**Examining Potential Re-renders**

I'm now considering the re-rendering of the `CreativeFunnel` component, triggered by the `onSaveToGallery` callback, which updates the `gallery` state in the parent `App` component. Since `CreativeFunnel` has a `key` prop tied to `funnelKey`, a change to `funnelKey` will cause a remount. However, even without a key change, changes to other props, like `wines` during the re-render could cause a crash if the component isn't stable. Specifically, I'm re-examining how a render triggered by a gallery update may be interacting with internal state changes within `CreativeFunnel` during a step transition.


**Identifying Lifecycle Errors**

I'm now focusing on a possible lifecycle or effect-related error that surfaces post-render, triggered by the "Render End" event. I suspect `atob` or other image processing is unlikely, since the error is in the UI. A blank screen, I'm now thinking, is more likely a component returning undefined, or crashing. The `renderStep5` conditional, specifically, and a blank screen, could point to that error, since the component re-renders if `state.imageHistory` is updated.


**Debugging the Loading State**

I'm now investigating potential crashes during the "converting to high quality image" phase. My current thinking is that the error likely occurs when the `loading` state transitions from `true` to `false` in `renderStep5`. I need to ensure that the component handles this state change robustly. I suspect the issue could be with `state.concepts`, which may be missing during the render, or a corrupted state.


**Refining Unmount Hypothesis**

I'm now reconsidering how the `CreativeFunnel` unmount might be the source of the render issue. I need to make sure `CreativeFunnel` is not unmounting due to the updated `AppScreen` state, when `onSaveToGallery` is called, but this doesn't seem to be it. I'm carefully reviewing the component lifecycle during the "Capturing the scene..." stage to identify any disruptions. I'm focusing on the interaction between the `App`'s state changes and the `CreativeFunnel`'s rendering.


**Implementing Robust Error Handling**

I'm now implementing an `ErrorBoundary` wrapper around `CreativeFunnel` to catch and manage potential rendering errors. I'll also safeguard the return statement of `CreativeFunnel` and ensure state updates are batched or handled in a fail-safe manner to prevent blank screens after a creative idea selection and high-quality image processing.


**Refining State Update Logic**

I'm now focusing on the simultaneous calls to `setState` and `onSaveToGallery` within `selectAndGenerate`, suspecting React batching could cause unmounts in specific React versions. I'll make the state updates atomic, prevent parent callbacks until local state is stable, and add checks for `state.imageHistory` in `renderStep5`. I'll also add a 'Panic' button if step is invalid. The issue might also come from `initialData` changes when the component is mounted.


**Clarifying Step Transition**

I'm now tracing a specific step transition, from 4 to 5, and suspect the crash during high-quality image conversion originates when accessing `state.imageHistory[0]` while `loading` is true and `step` is 5. It may come from a race condition between `loading` state updates and image history population, especially if initialData is provided.


**Resolving Gemini API Conflict**

I've discovered the root cause: an unsupported aspect ratio value sent to the Gemini API, specifically `'4:5'`, when Gemini only supports `'3:4'`. My type definitions were incorrect. I'm fixing the aspect ratio type and component logic to align with the supported values. This should resolve the rendering error.


**Addressing API Failures**

I'm now focusing on a potential error related to the Gemini API call returning `null`. Although I'm catching the error in `geminiService` and handling it, `generateFinalImage` is still being called. I'll correct the aspect ratios and component logic to match the supported values. I'll also check `selectAndGenerate` to ensure the error UI is correctly rendered when `img` is `null`. I'm concerned that re-renders triggered by `onSaveToGallery` might be the cause. Finally, I'm checking `renderStep5`.


**Verifying State Consistency**

I'm now investigating a discrepancy. Even after successful image generation, `state.imageHistory` might still be empty in `renderStep5`, resulting in a blank screen. This points to a potential issue with state updates within `selectAndGenerate` and its relationship with the image rendering process and the "Esperando señal de la cámara..." message.


**Refining Component Stability**

I'm now implementing more robust error boundaries within `CreativeFunnel` to display errors during image processing. I'm focusing on the component's unmount, which appears to be linked to state updates and the restart logic triggered by the top-left 'Funnel' action, specifically `handleBack` going back to the `HOME` screen. I'm defensively coding `renderStep5` to handle possible crashes.


**Stabilizing the "Back" Action**

I'm now investigating the inconsistent "Back" action behavior. It seems the issue is due to `CreativeFunnel` component remounting due to top-level state changes. I'm focusing on ensuring its stability during lifecycle events. I need to make the title update more consistent.


**Refactoring State Structure**

I'm now implementing atomic state updates. I've moved `isLoading` and `error` into the `FunnelState` object, which will help avoid state tearing. I'm also correcting the `AspectRatio` type to match Gemini API's allowed values. Finally, I'll update the component and `FunnelState` to improve the robustness of `renderStep5`, particularly its handling of the different loading and image states.

payload.text:
You are designing a conversational creative director app for wineries.

This is NOT a form.
This is a guided, intelligent, step-by-step creative conversation in Spanish.

GENERAL PRINCIPLES
- The app must feel like a creative professional asking natural questions.
- Questions must be progressive and conditional.
- Later questions depend on previous answers.
- Never show irrelevant options.
- Maintain a refined, minimal, editorial visual style (as in the previous working version).
- Language must be Spanish.
- No technical language exposed to the user.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 1 – WINE CONTEXT (FREE INPUT)

1. Ask the user which wine they want to work on.
   Free text input.

2. Ask: “¿Para quién va dirigido este vino?”
   Free text input (no buttons).

3. Ask: “¿Qué hace especial a este vino?”
   Free text input.

4. Ask optionally:
   “¿Quieres que analicemos la percepción pública de este vino en internet?”

   If YES:
   - Generate a public perception analysis based on social media, forums, press and reviews.
   - Explicitly exclude the winery’s own website.
   - Show a visible summary of TWO PARAGRAPHS.
   - Provide a button to download the full report as a text file.
   - Then ask:
     “¿Quieres que tengamos en cuenta este análisis o lo descartamos?”
     Buttons: “Tenerlo en cuenta” / “Descartarlo”

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 2 – VISUAL INTENT (CONDITIONAL LOGIC)

5. Ask what type of image they want:
   - Bodegón
   - Con personas
   - Sin personas
   - En uso / momento social

6. Ask for visual style (examples, not exhaustive):
   - Minimalista
   - Editorial
   - Natural
   - Lujoso
   - Cotidiano

CONDITIONAL RULES:
- If “Minimalista”:
  - Ask: “¿Muy minimal o minimal con algunos elementos?”
  - If “Muy minimal”: do NOT show object options.
  - If “Con elementos”: allow limited elements.

- If non-minimal:
  - Offer contextual elements (copas, uvas, corcho, botella, mesa, etc.)
  - Choices must match the wine’s positioning (luxury vs everyday).

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 3 – WHERE

7. Ask where the scene occurs:
   - Viñedo (default)
   - Bodega
   - Restaurante
   - Casa
   - Other

8. Allow the user to upload a reference image for the location (optional).

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 4 – WHEN (TIME CONTEXT)

9. Ask when the scene takes place.
   Suggestions must adapt to the current calendar:
   - Winter, Summer, Harvest, Valentine’s Day, Dinner with friends, etc.
   - Avoid out-of-season suggestions.

10. Allow free text input.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 5 – GENERATION

11. Generate creative concepts.
12. Let the user select one.
13. Generate the final image.

--------------------------------------------------
POST-GENERATION

- Allow Like / Dislike feedback.
- Store preferences internally.
- Do NOT enable advanced editing or variations yet.
- Stability is the priority.

CRITICAL:
- The app must be stable across repeated generations.
- Never show blank screens.
- Handle state safely.
- If something fails, show a clear message instead of crashing.

Goal:
A refined, intelligent creative assistant for wineries, not a form.

**Envisioning Conversational Flow**

I'm aiming to transform "Kinglab Bodegas 202" into a conversational experience. The goal is a Spanish-speaking, step-by-step "creative director" interaction, fundamentally changing the current form-based interface. Right now I'm focused on how users would naturally discuss managing wines, rather than just filling out forms.


**Defining Conversational Structure**

I've outlined a multi-phase conversational flow for Kinglab Bodegas 202. Phase 1 focuses on wine details. Phase 2 delves into visual intent, with image type, style, and conditional logic. Then, we have Phases 3 and 4 covering location and time context. Finally, Phase 5 generates concepts and images, followed by post-generation feedback.


**Updating Technical Implementation**

I'm now deep in code adjustments. I'm focusing on updating `types.ts` to reflect the new conversational structure and analysis requirements. I'm adding a function to `geminiService.ts` for public perception analysis, grounded via Google Search, excluding the winery's website. `CreativeFunnel.tsx` is undergoing a complete rewrite to handle the step-by-step logic, and `App.tsx` may need minor tweaks. I am also planning free text input for picking wines.

payload.text:
You are designing a professional AI-powered creative direction app for wineries.

This app is NOT a generic form.
This app is NOT a chatbot.
This app behaves like an experienced creative director guiding a winery step by step.

Language: Spanish.
Tone: professional, calm, clear, creative.
Visual style: refined, minimal, editorial, consistent with the previous stable version.

Stability is critical.
Never allow blank screens.
Never crash the UI.
If something fails, show a clear message and allow recovery.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 1 – PRODUCT REGISTRATION (STRUCTURED, NOT CONVERSATIONAL)

This phase must feel like “registering a product”, not a creative conversation.

Screen title: “Dar de alta un vino”

Fields:

1. Nombre del vino  
   Text input.

2. Imagen de la botella  
   Image upload (mandatory).
   This image represents the product reference.

3. ¿Para quién va dirigido este vino?  
   Free text input.

4. ¿Qué hace especial a este vino?  
   Free text input.

Once completed, confirm the product registration and continue.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 2 – DIGITAL PERCEPTION ANALYSIS (OPTIONAL, CLEAR)

Ask the user:

“¿Quieres que analicemos cómo se percibe este vino en el entorno digital?”

If the user says YES:

- Analyze public perception based on:
  - Social media
  - Forums
  - Reviews
  - Digital press
- Explicitly EXCLUDE the winery’s own website.

Results:

- Display a visible, readable summary of EXACTLY TWO PARAGRAPHS.
- This summary must always be shown on screen.
- Below the summary, add a button:
  “Descargar informe completo (DOC)”

Then ask:

“¿Quieres que tengamos en cuenta este análisis o lo descartamos?”

Buttons:
- Tenerlo en cuenta
- Descartarlo

If the user says NO:
- Skip this phase entirely.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 3 – CREATIVE DIRECTION (GUIDED & CONDITIONAL)

This phase must feel like a creative professional asking intelligent questions.

Questions must be progressive.
Options must adapt based on previous answers.
Never show irrelevant choices.

3.1 Tipo de imagen

Ask:
“¿Qué tipo de imagen quieres crear?”

Options:
- Bodegón
- Con personas
- Sin personas
- En uso / momento social

3.2 Estilo visual

Ask for the general style, for example:
- Minimalista
- Editorial
- Natural
- Lujoso
- Cotidiano

CONDITIONAL RULES:

If “Minimalista”:
- Ask:
  “¿Muy minimal o minimal con algunos elementos?”
- If “Muy minimal”:
  - Do NOT show object options (copas, uvas, corchos, etc.)
- If “Con elementos”:
  - Allow a limited, curated selection of elements.

If NOT minimal:
- Offer contextual elements (copas, uvas, corcho, botella, mesa, etc.)
- Elements must match the wine’s positioning:
  - Luxury wines → refined, premium elements
  - Everyday wines → simpler, natural elements

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 4 – WHERE (LOCATION)

Ask:
“¿Dónde ocurre la escena?”

Options:
- Viñedo (default)
- Bodega
- Restaurante
- Casa
- Otro entorno

Allow the user to upload a reference image for the location (optional).

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 5 – WHEN (TIME CONTEXT)

Ask:
“¿Cuándo ocurre la escena?”

Suggestions must adapt to the current calendar.
Avoid out-of-season options.

Examples:
- Invierno
- Vendimia
- San Valentín
- Verano
- Cena con amigos

Allow free text input.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 6 – DETAILS

Allow the user to add additional nuances:
Lighting, atmosphere, mood, texture, etc.
Free text input.

--------------------------------------------------
PHASE 7 – GENERATION

- Generate several creative concepts.
- Let the user choose one.
- Generate the final image.

--------------------------------------------------
POST-GENERATION BEHAVIOR

- Show the generated image clearly.
- Allow:
  - Me gusta
  - No me gusta
- Store preferences internally.

IMPORTANT LIMITATIONS (FOR STABILITY):

- Do NOT enable advanced image editing yet.
- Do NOT enable variations yet.
- Do NOT allow the app to reuse invalid state.

If the user wants to start again:
- Fully reset the creative flow safely.
- Never reuse previous generation state.

--------------------------------------------------
CRITICAL TECHNICAL RULES

- Protect all renders with safe state checks.
- Never render with null or undefined data.
- Always handle async image generation safely.
- Prevent React runtime errors and blank screens.

GOAL

A stable, elegant creative direction tool for wineries that feels professional,
intelligent and trustworthy.

**Defining Initial Scope**

I'm focusing on defining the initial scope for "Kinglab Bodegas 202". Right now, I'm concentrating on the "Product Registration" phase, specifically the "Dar de alta un vino" section. The core fields I'm considering are the wine's name, bottle image, target audience (which is currently free text), and any special notes. My next step will be to flesh out how those fields will be used.


**Expanding Feature Scope**

Now I'm diving deeper into the "Features" section. I'm introducing a digital perception analysis option, including social media and review analysis (excluding the winery's own site) and offering a downloadable summary report. I'm also adding image type, visual style, and location options, with conditional logic for the "Minimalista" style to refine element selections.


**Refining User Flow**

I'm now refining the user flow, focusing on the conversational logic within `CreativeFunnel.tsx`. I'm integrating options for reference image uploads and adding a time-based selection ("Winter," "Summer," etc.) with free text input for nuances. I'm also working on concept generation, user selection, and final image generation, with like/dislike feedback incorporated. Safety and stability, including error handling, are becoming a key focus. I'm also reviewing `App.tsx`, `types.ts`, `services/geminiService.ts`, `components/WineForm.tsx`, and `components/Layout.tsx`. The "Minimalista" style now considers price and positioning. Finally, I'm working to correctly calculate the current month for "Phase 5".


**Restructuring The Workflow**

I'm restructuring the initial workflow to integrate product registration. Now, the home screen leads to "Dar de alta un vino" (updated `WineForm.tsx`) if no wines are registered. Once registered, the user can "Comenzar Sesión" and the creative funnel starts (conversational style). I'm also refining `geminiService.ts`. The updated flow is: Home -> "Dar de alta un vino" -> "Comenzar Sesión" -> Funnel.

Call signature return types '{ visualStyle: FunnelState; step: number; selectedWine: Wine; analysisChoice: boolean; analysisResult: AnalysisResult; useAnalysis: boolean; imageType: ImageType; ... 11 more ...; feedback: "like" | "dislike"; }' and 'FunnelState' are incompatible.
The types of 'visualStyle' are incompatible between these types.
Type 'FunnelState' is not assignable to type 'string'.
This comparison appears to be unintentional because the types 'FunnelState' and 'string' have no overlap.

payload.text:
Fix the “Atmósfera” step so that atmosphere options are
explicitly visible and selectable in the UI.

Currently, atmosphere guidance exists conceptually,
but selectable options are not clearly rendered.

----------------------------------
ATMÓSFERA – UI VISIBILITY FIX
----------------------------------

1. In the “Atmósfera” step, render selectable buttons
that are clearly visible to the user.

2. Display the following options by default as buttons:
- Luz natural
- Luz suave / difusa
- Contraluz
- Iluminación dramática
- Ambiente cálido
- Ambiente frío / neutro

3. Add a clearly visible button:
“Más opciones”

4. When “Más opciones” is clicked, reveal additional buttons:
- Golden hour
- Luz lateral marcada
- Iluminación nocturna
- Ambiente íntimo
- Estética editorial
- Luz técnica / de estudio

5. Keep the free text input always visible:
“Describe la atmósfera o iluminación que imaginas”

----------------------------------
IMPORTANT
----------------------------------

- These must be real UI buttons, not implicit logic.
- The user must be able to click and select them.
- Selected atmosphere must directly affect lighting and mood.
- Do NOT change the order of steps.
- Do NOT modify other parts of the app.

Goal:
Make atmosphere choices visible, actionable,
and easy to understand for the user.

**Refining Atmósfera Stage**

I'm currently focused on refining the "Atmósfera" stage within the `CreativeFunnel` component, specifically Step 4. My objective is to enhance the user experience by providing explicit selectable buttons for lighting, alongside time, location, and the existing free-text "atmosphere" field. This addition is aimed at providing more direct control over the atmosphere customization.


**Expanding Atmosphere Options**

I've been expanding the lighting options in the "Atmósfera" step. I'm introducing selectable buttons for core lighting types such as natural and soft light, along with options for "Más opciones," which leads to further choices like "Golden hour" and "Night lighting." The existing free text input remains, but its label will be updated for clarity. I'm planning to store these new selections within the state, likely in a specific list or extending the existing `selections` record within the `CreativeFunnel` component, to differentiate technical specifications from contextual narrative.


**Implementing Atmosphere Selection**

I'm now implementing the selectable atmosphere buttons. I've decided to use a multi-select toggle, given the potential for overlapping selections like "natural" and "golden hour." I'll add a new key, `atmosfera`, to the `selections` object to store these choices as an array. This aligns with the "clearly visible" and "click and select" requirement. I'm focusing on integrating these new selections into the existing `CreativeFunnel.tsx` step 4 structure.


**Developing Atmosphere Options**

I'm now developing the lighting and atmosphere selection interface for Step 4. I'm building a grid of buttons, mirroring the style of other technical detail selections within the `CreativeFunnel` component. I'm adding local state for a "Más opciones" toggle, mimicking existing conditional displays. I'll store the atmosphere selections, including the core and expanded options, within the `selections` object, specifically under the new `atmosfera` key as an array. I'll need to check the code that renders Step 4, which is `renderStep4`, to integrate the interface there.


**Implementing Atmosphere Toggles**

I'm now implementing multi-select toggles for atmosphere choices in Step 4. I'm focusing on the "Más opciones" section, adding options like "Golden hour," "Luz lateral marcada," "Iluminación nocturna," "Ambiente íntimo," "Estética editorial," and "Luz técnica / de estudio." I'm also preparing for the XML generation. I've updated the `CreativeFunnel.tsx` state to include `showMoreAtmosphere`. My next step is (ii) Update. The state is safe to extend, since `selections` is passed as a string.


**Refining Handler Implementation**

I'm now refining the `handleSelection` function in `CreativeFunnel.tsx` to accommodate the multi-select nature of the "atmosfera" options. The previous implementation overwrites the values. I'm adding logic to handle the 'atmosfera' key, appending or removing values from the existing array within the state, instead of the original overwriting behavior. I will refactor to use an array. I'll need to double-check that the code renders the buttons properly.


**Deciding Atmosphere Implementation**

I'm now deciding how to handle atmosphere selections. Since some options represent lighting types and others moods, I've decided on a single selection rather than multi. The current implementation in Step 2 uses a single selection model. I'll stick with this approach for now, to keep it consistent.


**Updating Atmosphere Selection**

I'm now implementing toggleable buttons for atmosphere selection to accommodate multiple choices, since a combination like "Natural Light" and "Warm Atmosphere" is a common request. I've updated `CreativeFunnel.tsx` to handle a `showMoreAtmosphere` state and to update the `handleSelection` function. I'll focus on updating `renderStep4` next, and I need to ensure the existing code handles an array of selections correctly, given that `JSON.stringify(state.selections)` is used.


**Enhancing Atmosphere Display**

I'm now focusing on Step 4's interface. My recent focus has been on improving the "Atmósfera" selection options. I'm replacing the free-text `textarea` with a set of selectable buttons, specifically designed to offer explicit choices for atmosphere details like lighting and mood. I'm adding buttons for core options and a "Más opciones" button to expose more nuanced choices, which will be integrated in the `renderStep4` method. I'm aiming for a more structured selection process in `CreativeFunnel.tsx`.


**Evaluating Selection Handling**

I'm now evaluating the `handleSelection` function within `CreativeFunnel.tsx`. I need to ensure it supports multiple selections for the 'atmosfera' key. My plan is to modify the existing `handleSelection` logic to handle the 'atmosfera' selection as a toggle, instead of a single selection, while keeping single selection for others. I'm focusing on preserving consistency with Step 2, and ensuring the interface is responsive. I also want to rename `Cercanía` to `Valor de plano`. I'll verify the functionality.


**Implementing Atmosphere Buttons**

I'm now implementing the new atmosphere buttons within Step 4. I've added the core and extra options as toggleable buttons, and introduced local state `showMoreAtmosphere` to control the display of expanded choices. I'm focusing on ensuring proper state management for both core and extra selections, and the XML generation. I'm focusing on refactoring now to simplify.


**Refactoring Handler Logic**

I'm now refactoring the `handleSelection` function to accommodate new multi-select 'atmosfera' choices. I've integrated a conditional block within `handleSelection` to manage atmosphere selection. The logic correctly adds or removes options from an array in state. Next, I will refactor `renderStep4` to use a toggle button. This ensures a consistent experience.
